author: __ruebezahl__
content:
Hello all! 

I'm thrilled to share a tool I've built to translate a fuzzer friendly input format to a spec compliant ethereum block test. 
Those familiar with fuzzing via block tests knows how difficult it is due to the complexity of the format. This abstracts away everything and can be used to differentially fuzz EL client implementations.

All you need to do is provide:
1. pre-state (accounts, code, balance, etc etc) 
2. a list of blocks which contain transactions 
3. few environment fields (currrent random/difficulty etc ..) 

This will create a block test with accurate genesis and block headers. You can then run an EL client's block test command with the generated fixture and make sure they all agree on the test result.

Fuzzing with this library yielded two novel bugs (non security/exploitable, but still bugs) on Besu!
https://github.com/hyperledger/besu/issues/9840
https://github.com/hyperledger/besu/issues/9868

Happy to answer questions! Also, contributions are welcome.
https://github.com/R9295/blocktest-converter/
